rulings.Case Title: STONEBRAE CLUB PARTNERS, LLC VS. ROBERT WOLLACK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 2, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF STONEBRAE CLUB PARTNERS, LLC'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories is GRANTED. CCP 2030.270(b) requires that any extension of a deadline to respond to interrogatories specify the extended date. This did not occur. By operation of CCP 2030.290 the objections are waived. The later meet and confer correspondence did not preserve the right to object. Therefore, all objections are waived. Wollack's references to "any and all other damages" and "all other remedies" are improper because they do not identify what those damages or remedies are. While some types of damages may not be subject to precise calculation by a party (such as emotional distress damages assessed by the jury or financial estimates assessed by expert analysis), the party seeking those damages must identify them so the responding party can prepare. The request for sanctions is GRANTED in part. From the correspondence, it appears that the parties were able to narrow the issues from the motion to the reply. Additionally, in my discretion, awarding sanctions that exceed the amount in controversy would be unjust for purposes of CCP 2030.290(c). Balancing Stonebrae's success on the motion and the amount in controversy, I find that an award of $3,050 (5 hours at $610/hour) is appropriate. By December 20, 2019, Wollack shall pay sanctions and provide complete answers without objections to the remaining form interrogatories at issue. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DENTAL FORCE, INC. ET AL VS. BEMDAD BEHNOOD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 2, on Line 22, DEFENDANT BAMDAD BEHNOOD DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Bemdad Behnood's demurrer to the first amended complaint's (FAC) first cause of action is overruled. A personal services contract may be assigned when a merely technical change of ownership occurs. (Haldor, Inc. v. Beebe (1945) 72 Cal.App.2d 357.) The FAC alleges that Evangelista Dental Group, Inc. and plaintiff Evangelista Dental Group San Francisco 450 Sutter, PC are affiliates (id. at 9.), and the non-disclosure agreement provides that it applies to "affiliates." (NDA §6.) Thus, assignment may have been proper under Haldor and defendant's factual issues cannot be resolved on demurrer. Also, plaintiff alleges that defendant consented to the assignment. (FAC 17.) At most, defendant raises factual issues as to whether (1) the NDA applied to plaintiff and (2) defendant waived the NDA's written-modification term. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: CESAR HERNANDEZ SERRANO VS. SERVE WELL MARKET, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 2, on Line 7, CROSS DEFENDANT MATAGRANO, INC. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Cross-defendant Matagrano, Inc.'s demurrer to the cross-complaint is sustained with leave to amend. The form cross-complaint pleads comparative negligence as to plaintiff Cesar Serrano. However, Serrano's complaint does not sue for negligence; instead it pleads employment claims and failure to purchase workers compensation insurance, by which emotional distress was inflicted. The cross-complaint and complaint give no indication how Matagrano could be jointly liable on the claims Serrano has pled. rulings.Case Title: MINYING YANG VS. TRANSBAY JOINT POWERS AUTHORITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 2, on Line 9, DEFENDANT TRANSBAY JOINT POWERS AUTHORITY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Transbay Joint Powers Authority's (TJPA) demurrers to the first amended complaint's third, fourth and fifth causes of action are sustained with leave to amend. Public entities like the TJPA may be liable only if a statute so declares. (Cochran v. Herzog (1984) 155 Cal.App.3d 405, 409; Govt. Code §§815, 815.2.) Plaintiff relies on "Building Code Section 2406," which is a section heading in regulations. Section 2406 is not a statute and makes no declaration as to public entities. Further, "negligence per se" is not a cause of action (Quiroz v. Seventh Ave. Center (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1256, 1285), and no authority is cited for a "special relationship" between a transit passenger and the TJPA. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: QORUM, INC. VS. QAPROSOFT, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 2, on Line 10, DEFENDANT QAPROSOFT, LLC, SOLVD, INC., IGOR LYSENKO, IGOR VAYNER DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Moving Party: QAPROSOFT, LLC, SOLVD, INC., IGOR LYSENKO, IGOR VAYNER Filed: Oct 25, 2019 Tentative Ruling: The 12 demurrers filed by defendants QaProsoft, LLC; Solvd, Inc.; Igor Lysenko and Igor Vayner are all overruled: 1: A "plaintiff may plead the legal effect of the contract rather than its precise language." (Construction Protective Services, Inc. v. TIG Specialty Ins. Co. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 189, 199.) Moreover, defendants claim no lack of clarity regarding the contract. (Id.) 2: Alter ego is adequately pled against the individual defendants. (Cmplt. 2:19-3:19.) 3: Promissory fraud is adequately pled; e.g., the complaint alleges that defendants promised Lysenko "would perform backend software development services," but "did not intend to perform" and "did not perform" on the promise; i.e., it was never intended that Lysenko would do the promised work. (Cmplt. 7:10-28.) 4: Intentional fraud is adequately pled; the complaint alleges that defendants' monthly invoices contained knowingly false entries. (Cmplt. 8:4-22.) 5&6: While a plaintiff may not recover in tort for mere contract breaches, plaintiff has adequately pled well-recognized tort theories. 7: It is alleged that defendants intended to disrupt plaintiff's contracts with third-parties. (Cmplt. 8:23-9:75.) This suffices at the pleading stage. 8: The elements of intentional and negligent interference with prospective economic advantage are pled. (CACI 2202, 2204; Cmplt. 9:11-10:16.) 9&10: Contract and tort theories - which often measure damages differently - may be pled together or in the alternative. 11: Whether civil conspiracy is a cause of action or a doctrine, it is adequately pled. (Cmplt. 10:17-11:11.) The individual defendants may be liable for conspiracy. (Doctor's Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 49 Cal.3d 39, 47.) 12: A Business & Professions Code §17200 violation need not be pled with specificity. (Quelimane Co. v. Stewart Title Guaranty Co. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 26, 46-47.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu
